evals.prompt: I have an Adams model with a slider (marker 5 on part 2, ground marker is 1). I need to write a user subroutine in C that grabs the Y displacement once per iteration and makes it available to my VFOSUB so I don't recompute it every time the force is evaluated. Can you write the CBKSUB and show me how the VFOSUB would read the cached value?
evals.expected_output: A C CBKSUB that caches Y-displacement via c_sysary at ev_ITERATION_BEG using a file-scope global, plus a companion VFOSUB that reads the cache when valid and falls back to a direct SYSARY call otherwise. Both use correct SDK headers and enum constants.
expectations: CBKSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsCbksub* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params
CBKSUB includes adams_c_Callback forward declaration typedef
CBKSUB uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant, not a raw integer like case 4
CBKSUB handles ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 (ignores them without reading data[])
CBKSUB includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
VFOSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter) or correct pointer-based signature
VFOSUB includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
VFOSUB is dflag-aware: uses cache when dflag==0, falls back to direct SYSARY when dflag==1
Both files use c_errmes for error handling
Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
Agent offers to compile the code (or runs the build) rather than only providing manual build instructions
Manual Start Menu / Adams Command Prompt instructions are presented as a fallback for when agent compilation fails, not as the primary workflow

evals.prompt: In my Adams CBKSUB, I want to call my VFOSUB at the start of each iteration to pre-calculate the forces. What's the best way to do that in C?
evals.expected_output: The agent should warn that calling VFOSUB from CBKSUB is forbidden and suggest the correct alternative: cache values in CBKSUB via globals/shared state, then read them from VFOSUB.
expectations: Explicitly warns that VFOSUB cannot be called from within CBKSUB
Does NOT present calling VFOSUB directly from CBKSUB as a valid primary approach
Suggests caching pattern: compute in CBKSUB via utility functions, read from VFOSUB via shared state
Uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant (not raw integer) when showing the caching alternative
Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc (allowed utility routines) for computation inside CBKSUB

evals.prompt: I need a C user subroutine for an Adams VFORCE that applies a linear spring: Fx = -500*(DX - 100). The moving marker is 10, ground is marker 1. I need the whole thing - headers, the function, everything so I can compile it into a DLL.
evals.expected_output: A complete, compilable C VFOSUB with correct Adams SDK includes, the adams_c_Vfosub typedef forward declaration, iflag guard pattern (skip 5/7/9), c_sysary DISP call, spring force computation, error handling via c_errmes, and a build command.
expectations: Uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params with trailing underscore
Includes adams_c_Vfosub forward declaration typedef
Includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
Includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc to query displacement (not a raw function call)
Uses c_errmes for error handling after utility function calls
Parameterizes markers or constants via PAR[] instead of hardcoding marker IDs in the function body
Build command uses correct library name adams_util.lib (not adams_utils.lib)
Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
Agent offers to compile the code (or runs the build) rather than only providing manual build instructions
Manual Start Menu / Adams Command Prompt instructions are presented as a fallback for when agent compilation fails, not as the primary workflow
